events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes an "open ground" condition on receptacles & switches in a commercial electrical project at rough-complete stage. This is a defect/non-compliance issue.

events.label: response
events.text:

  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Receptacle/switch wiring exhibits an open ground condition at rough-complete stage",
    "Equipment grounding conductor not properly connected or continuous, creating shock/ground-fault hazard",
    "Non-compliant with NEC 406.4 general installation requirements and 406.9 boxes requiring effective grounding path"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify equipment grounding conductor continuity from device to panel and bond terminations",
    "Correct open ground at affected receptacle/switch before close-in",
    "Re-test with circuit tester to confirm ground path continuity"
  ],
  "rationale": "An open ground on installed receptacles/switches at rough-complete is an installed-defective, non-compliant condition. NEC 406.4 and 406.9 require receptacles to be effectively grounded and installed in accordance with applicable provisions; an open ground defeats fault-current return paths and GFCI/AFCI protection referenced under NEC 210.8, posing a high shock hazard.",
  "references": ["NEC 210.8", "NEC 406.4", "NEC 406.9"]
}
